EXHAUSTIVE_COUNT.diagnostic: total_triples (3816) equals re-extracted (claim × cite-key) pair count from content/*.md across all 14 section files.

CLAIM_SENTENCES_IN_FILES.diagnostic: All 3816 claim_text values are substrings of their cited section_filename after whitespace normalization.

CITE_KEYS_IN_BIB.diagnostic: All 414 unique cite_keys resolve to references.bib entries; all have non-empty DOI.

DOI_CONSISTENCY.diagnostic: All triples' bib_doi values match the DOI in references.bib for the corresponding cite_key (case-insensitive).

SECTION_FILE_RESOLVES.diagnostic: All section_filename values resolve to files in manuscript/content/.

NO_DUPLICATE_TRIPLES.diagnostic: triple_id uniqueness: all 3816 triple_ids are unique. (filename, paragraph_idx, sentence_idx, cite_key) tuples have 246 multi-occurrence entries; all of these correspond verbatim to sentences where the same cite_key appears multiple times in the source markdown (verified by re.findall on each claim_text). They are required by EXHAUSTIVE_COUNT, which mandates one triple per cite-key occurrence; deduplicating would violate EXHAUSTIVE_COUNT (3816 → 3570). Treated as legitimate same-sentence cite repeats.

BATCH_INTEGRITY.diagnostic: All 3816 triples assigned to exactly one of 212 batches; every batch contains 18 triples (≤18 skill limit, ≤25 orchestrator spec).

NON_EMPTY_SENTENCES.diagnostic: All 3816 claim_text values are non-empty after stripping.

SCHEMA_COMPLETENESS.diagnostic: All triples carry the full required schema (triple_id, section_filename, paragraph_idx, sentence_idx, claim_text, cite_key, expected_support_role, bib_doi, bib_title, bib_authors, bib_year, bib_venue, batch_id).

UNIQUE_KEY_COUNTS.diagnostic: Reported unique_cite_keys (414) and unique_paper_dois (414) match observed.

summary_notes: Phase 15 citation-triple extraction PASSES all structural validation checks. EXHAUSTIVE_COUNT confirms total_triples=3816 equals the cite-occurrence count re-extracted from content/*.md, with per-section counts matching exactly. All 3816 claim_text strings appear verbatim in their cited section file (whitespace-normalized), all 414 unique cite_keys resolve to references.bib entries with non-empty DOIs that match the triples' bib_doi field, and all 212 batches are exactly 18 triples (≤25 spec limit). triple_id uniqueness holds; the 246 (file, para, sent, cite_key) tuple repeats are bona-fide same-sentence cite re-occurrences in the source manuscript and are required by the EXHAUSTIVE_COUNT rule.
